instructions.summary: Audit and fix async cancellation safety in io_uring operations
instructions.objective: Fix potential use-after-free and connection leaks when futures are dropped during io_uring operations. Ensure all I/O ops are safely cancellable.
instructions.constraints: - Use monoio's cancellable I/O traits - No UAF under miri/valgrind - No fd leaks under load - All timeout paths audited - Document cancellation safety patterns

context.tree: harrow-server-monoio/ ├── src/ │ ├── lib.rs │ ├── connection.rs │ └── codec.rs └── tests/ └── integration.rs

guidance.notes:
CRITICAL: This is a safety issue. Read references first.
Problem locations: 1. lib.rs:99 - select! { accept vs shutdown } - accept future dropped 2. lib.rs:133 - shutdown during accept - connection leak 3. connection.rs:30 - connection timeout - inner future dropped 4. connection.rs:176 - header read timeout - read op cancelled
Solution pattern: ```rust let op = stream.read_cancellable(buf); select! { r = op.fuse() => r, _ = timeout => { op.cancel().await?; // Explicit kernel cancellation return Err(Timeout); } } ```
Testing: - Create test with rapid timeout/cancel cycles - Check /proc/self/fd for leaks - Run under miri if possible
